1: I’ve been struck by the fact that when Isaiah received his charge from the Lord, he bemoaned that he was “a man of unclean lips” and dwelt “in the midst of a people of unclean lips” (Isaiah 6:5). This sin too had to be purged from Isaiah if he was to bear the word of the Lord. Is it any wonder that psalmists and prophets alike have beseeched the Lord to “set a watch” before their lips and to guard the “door” of their lips (Psalm 141:3), to help them “sin not with [their] tongue”? (Psalm 39:1). (Robert S. Wood, in Conference Report, Oct. 1999, 108; or Ensign, Nov. 1999, 84)
